Usage: postman request [METHOD] <URL> [options]

Send HTTP requests from the command line with full Postman capabilities
including authentication, environment variables, assertions, retries, and
detailed output control.

Arguments:
  methodOrUrl                     HTTP method (GET, POST, PUT, DELETE, PATCH,
                                  HEAD, OPTIONS) or URL. If URL provided
                                  without method, defaults to GET.
  url                             The target URL to request (required if first
                                  argument is HTTP method)

Options:
  -H, --header <header>           Add HTTP header in key:value format. Can be
                                  used multiple times. Example: -H
                                  "Content-Type:application/json" -H
                                  "X-API-Key:abc123" (default: [])
  -d, --body <body>               Request body content. Supports: inline
                                  string, @filepath for file content, or "-"
                                  for stdin. Example: --body '{"name":"John"}'
                                  or --body @data.json
  -f, --form <field>              Add multipart/form-data field in key=value
                                  format. Can be used multiple times. For file
                                  uploads use key=@filepath. Example: -f
                                  "name=John" -f "avatar=@photo.jpg" (default:
                                  [])
  -e, --environment <path>        Load Postman Environment by UUID or file
                                  path. Variables in URL, headers, and body
                                  will be resolved. Example: -e
                                  dev.postman_environment.json
  --auth-<type>-<param> <value>   [Optional] Supply authentication parameters dynamically.
                                     Use --auth-<type>-<param> where <type> is the auth method and <param> is the parameter.
                                     Supported auth types: basic, bearer, digest, oauth1, oauth2, hawk, aws, ntlm, apikey
                                     Examples:
                                       Basic Auth: --auth-basic-username user --auth-basic-password pass
                                       Bearer Token: --auth-bearer-token mytoken123
                                       API Key: --auth-apikey-key "X-API-Key" --auth-apikey-value "abc123" --auth-apikey-in header
                                       AWS: --auth-aws-accessKey KEY --auth-aws-secretKey SECRET --auth-aws-service s3
                                     Type: string, Default: null
  --timeout <n>                   Request timeout in milliseconds. Request will
                                  fail if no response within this time.
                                  (default: 300000)
  --redirects-ignore              Prevents Postman CLI from automatically
                                  following 3XX redirect responses. By default,
                                  redirects are followed.
  --redirects-max <n>             Maximum number of redirects to follow.
                                  Default is unlimited. Useful for preventing
                                  redirect loops.
  --redirects-follow-method       Preserves the original HTTP method when
                                  following redirects. By default, redirects
                                  are followed with GET method.
  --redirects-remove-referrer     Removes the Referer header when following
                                  redirects. By default, Referer header is sent
                                  with redirects.
  --retry <n>                     Number of retry attempts for failed requests
                                  (4xx, 5xx, network errors). Useful for flaky
                                  endpoints or rate-limited APIs. (default: 0)
  --retry-delay <ms>              Delay between retry attempts in milliseconds.
                                  (default: 1000)
  --script-pre-request <script>   Add JavaScript script to run before the
                                  request. Can be inline JS or @filepath to
                                  load from file. Scripts have access to pm.*
                                  APIs. Example: --script-pre-request
                                  "pm.environment.set('timestamp',
                                  Date.now());" or --script-pre-request
                                  @setup.js
  --script-post-request <script>  Add JavaScript script to run after the
                                  request. Can be inline JS or @filepath to
                                  load from file. Scripts have access to pm.*
                                  APIs. Example: --script-post-request
                                  "console.log(pm.response.json());" or
                                  --script-post-request @validate.js
  -o, --output <path>             Save complete response to JSON file.Useful
                                  for debugging or further processing.
  --verbose                       Show detailed request and response
                                  information including headers, body, and
                                  metadata.
  --response-only                 suppress all output except response body.
                                  Useful for piping to other commands.
  --debug                         Debug mode: show detailed execution
                                  information including retry attempts,
                                  redirects, and timing breakdowns.
  -h, --help                      display help for command

Examples:

  Basic Requests:
    Simple GET request (method defaults to GET):
      postman request https://api.example.com/users

    GET request with explicit method:
      postman request GET https://api.example.com/users

    POST request with JSON body:
      postman request POST https://api.example.com/users \
        --body '{"name": "Alice", "email": "alice@example.com"}'

    PUT request with body from file:
      postman request PUT https://api.example.com/users/123 --body @user-data.json

    DELETE request:
      postman request DELETE https://api.example.com/users/123

  Authentication:
    Bearer token authentication:
      postman request https://api.example.com/protected \
        --auth-bearer-token eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9...

    Basic authentication:
      postman request https://api.example.com/protected \
        --auth-basic-username myuser \
        --auth-basic-password mypass

    API Key in header:
      postman request https://api.example.com/data \
        --auth-apikey-key "X-API-Key" \
        --auth-apikey-value "abc123xyz" \
        --auth-apikey-in header

    API Key in query parameter:
      postman request https://api.example.com/data \
        --auth-apikey-key "apikey" \
        --auth-apikey-value "abc123xyz" \
        --auth-apikey-in query

    AWS Signature v4 authentication:
      postman request https://s3.amazonaws.com/my-bucket/object \
        --auth-aws-accessKey AKIAIOSFODNN7EXAMPLE \
        --auth-aws-secretKey wJalrXUtnFEMI/K7MDENG/bPxRfiCYEXAMPLEKEY \
        --auth-aws-service s3 \
        --auth-aws-region us-east-1

  Headers and Forms:
    Add custom headers:
      postman request https://api.example.com/data \
        -H "Content-Type:application/json" \
        -H "X-Custom-Header:custom-value" \
        -H "Accept:application/json"

    Multipart form data (file upload):
      postman request POST https://api.example.com/upload \
        -f "file=@document.pdf" \
        -f "title=My Document" \
        -f "description=Important file"

    Form data with multiple files:
      postman request POST https://api.example.com/gallery \
        -f "photos=@image1.jpg" \
        -f "photos=@image2.jpg" \
        -f "album_name=Vacation 2025"

  Environment Variables:
    Use Postman environment file:
      postman request https://api.example.com/{{endpoint}} \
        -e production.postman_environment.json \
        -H "Authorization:Bearer {{auth_token}}"

    Environment with authentication:
      postman request POST https://{{base_url}}/api/data \
        -e dev.postman_environment.json \
        --body '{"userId": "{{user_id}}", "data": "test"}'

  Pre-Request and Post-Request Scripts:
    Inline pre-request script (set timestamp before request):
      postman request https://api.example.com/data \
        --script-pre-request "pm.environment.set('timestamp', Date.now());"

    Inline post-request script (log response):
      postman request https://api.example.com/users \
        --script-post-request "console.log('Response:', pm.response.json());"

    Load pre-request script from file:
      postman request https://api.example.com/data \
        --script-pre-request @setup.js

    Load post-request script from file:
      postman request https://api.example.com/users \
        --script-post-request @validate.js

    Combine both pre-request and post-request scripts (inline):
      postman request POST https://api.example.com/orders \
        --body '{"product": "widget", "quantity": 5}' \
        --script-pre-request "pm.environment.set('requestTime', new Date().toISOString());" \
        --script-post-request "const order = pm.response.json(); console.log('Order created:', order.id);"

    Combine both scripts (from files):
      postman request POST https://api.example.com/process \
        --body @data.json \
        --script-pre-request @setup.js \
        --script-post-request @teardown.js

    Use scripts for dynamic authentication (inline):
      postman request https://api.example.com/protected \
        --script-pre-request "pm.request.headers.add({key: 'X-Request-ID', value: Date.now().toString()});" \
        --script-post-request "console.log('Status:', pm.response.code);"

    Advanced post-request script with validation (from file):
      postman request https://api.example.com/users/123 \
        --script-post-request @validate-user-response.js

    Example setup.js file content:
      // Set dynamic timestamp
      pm.environment.set('timestamp', Date.now());
      // Add custom header
      pm.request.headers.add({
        key: 'X-Request-Time',
        value: new Date().toISOString()
      });

    Example validate.js file content:
      // Validate response structure
      const response = pm.response.json();
      if (!response.id) {
        console.error('Response missing required id field');
      }
      console.log('Validation passed for:', response.id);

  Retry Logic:
    Retry failed requests up to 3 times:
      postman request https://api.flaky-service.com/data \
        --retry 3 \
        --retry-delay 2000

    Retry with debug output to see all attempts:
      postman request https://api.example.com/data \
        --retry 5 \
        --retry-delay 1000 \
        --debug

  Output Control:
    Save complete response to JSON file (status, headers, body, timings):
      postman request https://api.example.com/data --output response.json

    Download binary file using response only mode and shell redirection:
      postman request https://cdn.example.com/archive.zip --response-only > archive.zip

    Response only mode - only output response body (useful for piping):
      postman request https://api.example.com/data --response-only | jq '.results[]'

    Verbose mode - show all request/response details:
      postman request https://api.example.com/data --verbose

    Debug mode - show execution details, redirects, timing:
      postman request https://api.example.com/data --debug

  Advanced Use Cases:
    Complete CI/CD health check with validation:
      postman request https://api.production.com/health \
        --timeout 5000 \
        --retry 3 \
        --retry-delay 1000 \
        --script-post-request "pm.test('Health check status', function() { \
          const resp = pm.response.json(); \
          pm.expect(resp.status).to.equal('healthy'); \
        });" \
        --response-only

    Long-running request with complete response capture:
      postman request https://api.example.com/long-process \
        --output response.json \
        --timeout 300000 \
        --verbose

    Chain requests using jq and environment variables:
      TOKEN=$(postman request POST https://api.example.com/login \
        --body '{"username":"admin","password":"pass"}' \
        --response-only | jq -r '.token')

      postman request https://api.example.com/protected \
        --auth-bearer-token "${TOKEN}" \
        --script-post-request "console.log('Authorized access granted');"

    Request with environment variables and post-processing:
      postman request POST https://{{base_url}}/api/users \
        -e dev.postman_environment.json \
        --body '{"name": "{{test_user}}", "role": "admin"}' \
        --script-post-request "const user = pm.response.json(); console.log('Created user with ID:', user.id);"

  Usage Notes:
    - Quick API testing without leaving the terminal
    - CI/CD pipelines with pre/post-request scripts for dynamic setup and validation
    - Automated health checks and monitoring
    - Replacing curl/wget with Postman's powerful features
    - Testing APIs with complex authentication schemes
    - Integration with Postman Environments for multi-environment testing
    - Use pre-request scripts to set dynamic values, headers, or environment variables
    - Use post-request scripts to process responses, log data, or validate results

  Exit Codes:
    0: Success (2xx-3xx response, all tests passed)
    N: Number of failed tests (e.g., exit code 3 means 3 tests failed)

  Piping Output:
    postman request https://api.example.com/users --response-only | jq '.[] | .name'
